content.claim: As of 2024/25, Hungary has 880 secondary general-education (gimnázium) schools nationally, with eight-year programmes starting from grade 5 and six-year programmes from grade 7.
content.context: International difference-in-differences evidence indicates that delaying selection from age 10–12 to age 14 would likely reduce Hungary's socioeconomic achievement gap by approximately 0.15–0.25 SD (based on Polish comparable gains), but this would require compensatory instructional resources and teacher supply that Hungary currently lacks in demonstrable quantity.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
